context: Tests whether the agent refuses to accept vague requirements like 'highly available' without quantification, asks for specific numbers, and specifically probes the 99.9% vs 99.99% distinction for availability — rather than filling in assumptions and proceeding.

checklist.name: Does not assume availability target
checklist.description: review.md does NOT assume or define what 'highly available' means and proceed — it asks the question instead

checklist.name: Asks for specific availability number
checklist.description: review.md asks for a specific availability percentage, SLA, or uptime target rather than accepting 'highly available' as sufficient

checklist.name: 99.9% vs 99.99% distinction
checklist.description: review.md references the difference between 99.9% and 99.99% uptime (or the equivalent downtime hours: 8.7 hours vs 52 minutes per year)

checklist.name: Does not assume performance target
checklist.description: review.md does NOT assume what 'fast enough' or similar vague performance terms mean — it asks for specific numbers (e.g. latency in ms, throughput in RPS)

checklist.name: No architecture proposed yet
checklist.description: review.md does NOT propose or begin describing an architecture before the vague requirements are resolved
